esteem. Though he (Varaha) is conscious of the fact that he is
treating of scientific topics, yet the poet in him asserts himself and
makes his work a work of art too, a piece of excellent poetry — a
Mahdkavya—t'which is a joy forever.' He is an adept in embellishing
his statements with apt figures of speech. He is seen wielding mastery
over the figures in the following instances :
1. Alliteration : XXIV. 1, 2, 18, 36; XXVI. 8; XXX. 6; XII, I,
2, 7; XIX. 2,4, 17, 18;XLIII. 7. 35, 38, 39; XLVH. 9; LXIV.
3; LXXI. 1-7; LXXVI. 6, 9; LXXVU. 36; LXXVUI. 2>
LXXIV. 3; LXVIH. 37, 39, 43, 44, 47, 109; LXIX. 37;
LXXIX. 27; LXXXI. 7, 21; LXXX1I. 5; XCIV. 14; CI. 12.
2. General Statement {Arthantaranydsa). II. 2; LIU. 95; LXX- 23;
LXXV. 1-4, LXXXVHI. 14.
3. Kdvyalinga : II. 24, 28, 29, 33; LXXIV. 2, 5; LXXV. 4.
4. Dr$tanta ; XLVII. 28.
5. Sdrdlatfikdra: LXXIV. 1.
6. Svabhdvokti : XXVIII. 4, 5.
7. Tathdsamkhya : XLIII. 5.
8. Pratipa : LXXIV. 2.
9. Vyaiireka : II, 38, 40; LXXIV. 2.
